description: Extract shared frontmatter parser from migrate/skill_common.rs into libaipm::frontmatter module
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/frontmatter.rs with Frontmatter struct (fields: BTreeMap<String, String>, start_line: usize, end_line: usize, body: String)
Implement pub fn parse(content: &str) -> Option<Frontmatter> using line-by-line scanning for --- delimiters (matching regex pattern from both CLIs: /^---\s*\n([\s\S]*?)---\s*\n?/)
Handle key-value extraction, multi-line values (hooks: blocks with indented continuation), and YAML quote stripping via strip_yaml_quotes()
Add pub mod frontmatter to crates/libaipm/src/lib.rs
Refactor skill_common::parse_skill_frontmatter() to delegate to frontmatter::parse()
Refactor agent_detector, command_detector, output_style_detector, copilot_agent_detector to use shared parser
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all existing migrate tests still pass
Add unit tests: valid frontmatter, missing closing ---, no frontmatter, empty content, multi-line values, quote stripping
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check
Verify branch coverage >= 89% for frontmatter.rs

description: Core lint infrastructure: LintRule trait, Diagnostic struct, Severity enum, LintOutcome struct, lint Error type
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/ directory structure: mod.rs, rule.rs, diagnostic.rs
Implement Severity enum with Warning and Error variants, derive Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord (Warning < Error)
Implement Diagnostic struct with fields: rule_id (String), severity (Severity), message (String), file_path (PathBuf), line (Option<usize>), source_type (String)

Implement LintRule trait (Send + Sync) with methods: id() -> &'static str, name() -> &'static str, default_severity() -> Severity, check(source_dir: &Path, fs: &dyn Fs) -> Result<Vec<Diagnostic>, Error>
Implement LintOutcome struct with fields: diagnostics (Vec<Diagnostic>), error_count (usize), warning_count (usize), sources_scanned (Vec<String>)
Create lint Error enum using thiserror with appropriate variants (Io, Parse, etc.)
Add pub mod lint to crates/libaipm/src/lib.rs
Add unit tests for Severity ordering (Warning < Error), Diagnostic construction
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Lint configuration: LintConfig parsing from [workspace.lints] in aipm.toml (Cargo-style)
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/config.rs
Implement LintConfig struct with ignore_paths (Vec<String>) and rule_overrides (BTreeMap<String, RuleOverride>)
Implement RuleOverride enum: Level(Severity) for simple 'skill/missing-description' = 'error', Detailed { level: Severity, ignore: Vec<String> } for inline table syntax

Implement serde Deserialize types: WorkspaceLints with #[serde(flatten)] for rules BTreeMap and ignore field, LintRuleValue untagged enum (Simple string or Detailed table), LintIgnore struct with paths field
Add optional lints field (Option<WorkspaceLints>) to the existing Workspace struct in manifest/types.rs
Implement LintConfig::from_workspace_lints() converting deserialized TOML into runtime config
Implement LintConfig::default() returning empty config (all defaults apply)
Implement override resolution: 'allow' skips rule entirely, level override changes severity, else default_severity()
Implement glob matching for ignore paths (global and per-rule) relative to workspace root using glob crate
Add unit tests: simple overrides, detailed overrides with ignore, 'allow' suppression, empty config, invalid severity string, glob pattern matching, per-rule ignore paths
Run cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89% for config.rs

description: Reporters: TextReporter and JsonReporter for human-readable and JSON lint output
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/reporter.rs
Implement Reporter trait with fn report(outcome: &LintOutcome, writer: &mut dyn Write) -> Result<(), Error>

Implement TextReporter matching clippy/rustc format: 'severity[rule_id]: message
  --> file:line
  |
  = help: ...'
Implement summary line: 'X error(s), Y warning(s) emitted' or 'no issues found' for clean pass
Implement JsonReporter producing { diagnostics: [...], summary: { errors, warnings, sources_scanned } } via serde_json::to_writer_pretty
Use std::io::Write with write!/writeln! for all output (never println! per CLAUDE.md)
Add unit tests: TextReporter format matches expected output, summary line, empty diagnostics ('no issues found')
Add unit tests: JsonReporter produces valid JSON, correct structure, empty case
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89% for reporter.rs

description: Known events module: hard-coded tool-specific hook event lists from binary analysis
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/known_events.rs

Define CLAUDE_EVENTS const array with 26 PascalCase events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure, Notification, SessionStart, Stop, StopFailure, SubagentStart, SubagentStop, PreCompact, PostCompact, SessionEnd, PermissionRequest, Setup, TeammateIdle, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, UserPromptSubmit, ToolError, Elicitation, ElicitationResult, ConfigChange, InstructionsLoaded, WorktreeCreate, WorktreeRemove, CwdChanged, FileChanged
Define COPILOT_EVENTS const array with 10 camelCase events: sessionStart, sessionEnd, userPromptSubmitted, preToolUse, postToolUse, errorOccurred, agentStop, subagentStop, subagentStart, preCompact
Define COPILOT_LEGACY_MAP const array of (legacy_name, canonical_name) tuples for PascalCase -> camelCase mapping
Add helper fn is_valid_event(event: &str, tool: &str) -> bool
Add helper fn suggest_canonical(event: &str) -> Option<&'static str> for legacy name suggestions
Add unit tests for each helper function
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Marketplace lint rule: skill/missing-name — SKILL.md missing name field in frontmatter
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_missing_name.rs
Implement SkillMissingNameRule as zero-sized unit struct
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'skill/missing-name', default_severity() returns Warning
In check(): scan source_dir for plugin subdirectories, then skills/<name>/SKILL.md files via Fs::read_dir()
Use frontmatter::parse() to extract frontmatter fields
If 'name' field is absent, produce Diagnostic with file_path and line 1
Accept &dyn Fs for all filesystem access
Add unit tests with MockFs: SKILL.md with name (no finding), without name (finding), no frontmatter (finding), multiple skills
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Marketplace lint rule: skill/missing-description — SKILL.md missing description field in frontmatter
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_missing_desc.rs
Implement SkillMissingDescriptionRule as zero-sized unit struct
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'skill/missing-description', default_severity() returns Warning
In check(): scan .ai/<plugin>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md files using &dyn Fs
Use frontmatter::parse() to check for 'description' field
If absent, produce Diagnostic with file_path and line 1 (note: Copilot auto-generates from body but quality is poor)
Add unit tests with MockFs: with description (no finding), without (finding), empty description string
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Marketplace lint rule: skill/oversized — SKILL.md exceeds 15,000 characters (Copilot SKILL_CHAR_BUDGET)
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_oversized.rs
Implement SkillOversizedRule as zero-sized unit struct
Define const SKILL_CHAR_BUDGET: usize = 15_000 (from Copilot CLI binary analysis)
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'skill/oversized', default_severity() returns Warning
In check(): read SKILL.md content via Fs::read_to_string(), check content.len() against SKILL_CHAR_BUDGET
If exceeds threshold, produce Diagnostic with character count in message
Add unit tests: small file (no finding), exactly at threshold, over threshold (finding), empty file
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Marketplace lint rule: agent/missing-tools — agent markdown missing tools frontmatter declaration
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/agent_missing_tools.rs
Implement AgentMissingToolsRule as zero-sized unit struct
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'agent/missing-tools', default_severity() returns Warning
In check(): scan .ai/<plugin>/agents/<name>.md files
Use frontmatter::parse() to check for 'tools' field
If absent, produce Diagnostic with file_path and line 1
Add unit tests: agent with tools (no finding), without tools (finding), no frontmatter, multiple agents
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Marketplace lint rule: hook/unknown-event — tool-aware hook event validation (26 Claude + 10 Copilot events)
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/hook_unknown_event.rs
Implement HookUnknownEventRule as zero-sized unit struct
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'hook/unknown-event', default_severity() returns Error
In check(): read hooks/hooks.json via Fs, parse as JSON using serde_json
Determine tool context from adapter (Claude vs Copilot) to select correct event list from known_events.rs
Extract event keys from the hooks object, validate each against the tool-specific event list
For marketplace (.ai/) plugins, validate against the union of both sets (accept any event valid in either tool)
For each unknown event, produce Diagnostic with line number and valid events in help message
Add unit tests: valid Claude events (no finding), valid Copilot events (no finding), unknown event (finding with valid list), multiple unknowns, malformed JSON, missing file
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Marketplace lint rule: plugin/broken-paths — broken file references in plugin markdown and components
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/broken_paths.rs
Implement BrokenPathsRule as zero-sized unit struct
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'plugin/broken-paths', default_severity() returns Error
In check(): scan all markdown files in .ai/<plugin>/
Extract ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/ and ${SKILL_DIR}/ references from file content
Resolve references relative to the skill directory and check existence via Fs::exists()
Also check [components] paths in aipm.toml if present (parse manifest, check each component path exists)
For each broken reference, produce Diagnostic with source file path and line number
Add unit tests: valid references (no finding), broken script ref (finding), broken component path, no references, mixed valid/broken
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Cross-tool rule: skill/name-too-long — skill name exceeds 64 characters (Copilot Zod limit)
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_name_too_long.rs
Implement SkillNameTooLongRule as zero-sized unit struct
Define const MAX_SKILL_NAME_LENGTH: usize = 64 (from Copilot CLI Zod schema: z.string().max(64))
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'skill/name-too-long', default_severity() returns Warning
In check(): parse SKILL.md frontmatter, check 'name' field length against 64 char limit
If exceeds, produce Diagnostic with current length and limit in message
Add unit tests: short name (no finding), exactly 64 chars (no finding), 65 chars (finding), no name field (no finding)
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Cross-tool rule: skill/name-invalid-chars — skill name doesn't match Copilot regex
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_name_invalid.rs
Implement SkillNameInvalidCharsRule as zero-sized unit struct
Define the Copilot name regex: /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._\- ]*$/ (from Copilot CLI Zod schema)
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'skill/name-invalid-chars', default_severity() returns Warning
In check(): parse SKILL.md frontmatter, validate 'name' field against regex
If doesn't match, produce Diagnostic with invalid characters highlighted
Note: Copilot replaces spaces with hyphens post-parse, so spaces are valid
Add unit tests: valid name (no finding), starts with special char (finding), contains invalid chars (finding), no name field (no finding)
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Cross-tool rule: skill/description-too-long — description exceeds 1024 characters (Copilot Zod limit)
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_desc_too_long.rs
Implement SkillDescriptionTooLongRule as zero-sized unit struct
Define const MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH: usize = 1024 (from Copilot CLI Zod schema: z.string().max(1024))
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'skill/description-too-long', default_severity() returns Warning
In check(): parse SKILL.md frontmatter, check 'description' field length against 1024 char limit
If exceeds, produce Diagnostic with current length and limit in message
Add unit tests: short description (no finding), exactly 1024 chars (no finding), 1025 chars (finding), no description field (no finding)
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Cross-tool rule: skill/invalid-shell — shell frontmatter field not bash or powershell (Claude Code validation)
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_invalid_shell.rs
Implement SkillInvalidShellRule as zero-sized unit struct
Define const VALID_SHELLS: &[&str] = &['bash', 'powershell'] (from Claude Code binary analysis)
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'skill/invalid-shell', default_severity() returns Error
In check(): parse SKILL.md frontmatter, if 'shell' field present, validate against VALID_SHELLS (case-insensitive, trimmed)
If invalid, produce Diagnostic with the invalid value and valid options in message
Note: Claude Code silently falls back to bash; this rule surfaces the issue at lint time
Add unit tests: 'bash' (no finding), 'powershell' (no finding), 'zsh' (finding), 'Bash' uppercase (no finding), no shell field (no finding)
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Cross-tool rule: hook/legacy-event-name — PascalCase hook event that Copilot normalizes to camelCase
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/hook_legacy_event.rs
Implement HookLegacyEventNameRule as zero-sized unit struct
Use COPILOT_LEGACY_MAP from known_events.rs for PascalCase -> camelCase mapping
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'hook/legacy-event-name', default_severity() returns Warning
In check(): parse hooks/hooks.json, check each event key against COPILOT_LEGACY_MAP
If a legacy name is found, produce Diagnostic suggesting the canonical camelCase name
Include mapping: SessionStart->sessionStart, Stop->agentStop, UserPromptSubmit->userPromptSubmitted, etc.

Add unit tests: canonical names (no finding), legacy 'Stop' (finding suggesting 'agentStop'), legacy 'PostToolUseFailure' (finding suggesting 'errorOccurred'), non-legacy PascalCase Claude event (no finding)
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Source lint rule: source/misplaced-features — plugin features in .claude/ or .github/ instead of .ai/ marketplace
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/misplaced_features.rs
Implement MisplacedFeaturesRule struct with source_type field (&'static str)
Implement LintRule trait: id() returns 'source/misplaced-features', default_severity() returns Warning
In check(): scan source directory for known plugin artifact subdirectories (skills/, commands/, agents/, hooks/, output-styles/)
Check if .ai/ marketplace directory exists in the project root
If artifacts found and .ai/ exists, produce Diagnostic for each with suggestion to run aipm migrate
Handle both .claude and .github source types
Add unit tests: no artifacts (no finding), artifacts with .ai/ present (finding), artifacts without .ai/ (no finding), multiple artifact types
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: Rule factory functions, rules_for_source() dispatch, and rule module exports
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/mod.rs
Implement claude_lint_rules() returning Vec<Box<dyn LintRule>> with MisplacedFeaturesRule for .claude
Implement copilot_lint_rules() returning Vec<Box<dyn LintRule>> with MisplacedFeaturesRule for .github
Implement marketplace_lint_rules() returning Vec<Box<dyn LintRule>> with all 11 marketplace rules (6 core + 5 cross-tool)
Implement rules_for_source(source: &str) dispatch: '.claude' -> claude_lint_rules(), '.github' -> copilot_lint_rules(), '.ai' -> marketplace_lint_rules(), _ -> vec![]
Re-export all rule modules and known_events module
Add unit tests: each factory returns correct count (1, 1, 11), rules_for_source dispatch, unknown source returns empty
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Lint pipeline orchestrator: lint() entry point with parallel execution and config integration
steps: Implement lint::lint(opts: LintOptions, fs: &dyn Fs) -> Result<LintOutcome, Error> in crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs
Define LintOptions struct: dir (PathBuf), source filter (Option<String>), config (LintConfig), max_depth (Option<usize>)
Reuse migrate/discovery.rs to discover source directories with patterns ['.claude', '.github'] + always include '.ai'
Apply --source filter if provided
For each discovered source: call rules_for_source(), filter by config (remove 'allow'ed rules), apply severity overrides
Execute rules in parallel via rayon::par_iter() (matching migrate's parallelism model)
Check global and per-rule ignore paths before running each rule (ignore fast-path)
Collect all Vec<Diagnostic>, merge and sort by file_path
Count errors and warnings, build LintOutcome
Add unit tests with MockFs: clean workspace (no findings), workspace with issues, source filtering, config overrides, ignore paths, parallel execution
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: CLI integration: add Lint subcommand to aipm binary with cmd_lint() handler
steps: Add Lint variant to Commands enum in crates/aipm/src/main.rs with clap arg annotations: dir (default '.'), --source (Option<String>), --format (default 'text'), --max-depth (Option<usize>)
Add match arm in run() at the dispatch section that destructures Lint variant and calls cmd_lint()
Implement cmd_lint() handler: call resolve_dir(), load aipm.toml for [workspace.lints] config via manifest::parse(), build LintOptions, call libaipm::lint::lint()
Select reporter based on --format flag (TextReporter for 'text', JsonReporter for 'json')
Write output to stdout via reporter using std::io::stdout()
Return Ok(()) if outcome.error_count == 0, return appropriate error for exit code 1 if errors found
Handle error cases: write to stderr via writeln!
Run cargo build --workspace && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check

description: Integration tests: aipm lint end-to-end CLI tests with fixtures
steps: Create test fixtures for lint scenarios in fixtures/ directory: clean workspace, workspace with missing frontmatter, workspace with broken paths, workspace with unknown hook events, workspace with cross-tool violations
Add integration test: aipm lint on clean workspace returns exit 0 and 'no issues found'
Add integration test: aipm lint on workspace with errors returns exit 1 with correct diagnostics
Add integration test: aipm lint --source .claude only runs source rules (misplaced-features)
Add integration test: aipm lint --source .ai only runs marketplace rules
Add integration test: aipm lint --format json produces valid JSON output matching schema
Add integration test: config overrides with 'allow' suppress rules, severity overrides change output
Add integration test: global ignore paths exclude directories from all rules
Add integration test: per-rule ignore paths work correctly with inline table syntax
Add integration test: cross-tool rules fire for name-too-long, invalid-chars, description-too-long, invalid-shell, legacy-event-name
Run cargo test --workspace
Verify branch coverage >= 89% for all new code

description: BDD step definitions: wire up cucumber-rs steps for quality.feature lint scenarios (lines 25-49)
steps: Add step definitions for 'Given a skill package with a SKILL.md missing the description field' (create temp fixture)
Add step definitions for 'When the user runs aipm-pack lint' (use aipm lint for v1)
Add step definitions for 'Then a warning is reported: ...' matching exact message text from Diagnostic output
Add step definitions for 'Then an error is reported: ...' matching exact message text
Add step definitions for 'Then the command succeeds with no issues found'
Add step for scenario: Lint detects oversized SKILL.md (quality.feature:30) — create fixture with >15000 chars
Add step for scenario: Lint detects agent without tools declaration (quality.feature:35)
Add step for scenario: Lint validates hook event names (quality.feature:40) — use 'InvalidEvent' as unknown
Add step for scenario: Lint passes for well-formed plugin (quality.feature:46)
Run the BDD test suite and verify all lint scenarios pass
Verify branch coverage >= 89%
